value.reason: The page clearly documents the "Agent Teams" capability for Claude Code, confirming the label is accurate. However, the description contains an inaccuracy: it states agents "communicate directly with each other via peer-to-peer messaging," which misrepresents the architecture. According to the page, the team has one session acting as the "team lead" that coordinates work, assigns tasks, and synthesizes results — not a flat peer-to-peer structure. While teammates can message each other directly (distinct from subagents), the page emphasizes a lead-and-teammates hierarchy with a shared task list, not equal peer-to-peer communication. The description also omits that Agent Teams are experimental and disabled by default, which is a significant qualifier on the page. The phrase "peers" is also misleading given the explicit lead/teammate distinction drawn in the documentation.
